steps.title: Read the vertical asymptote to find $b$
steps.content: The visible branch of the graph drops without bound as $x$ approaches $-4$ from the left, so the vertical asymptote is $x=-4$. For $f(x)=\frac{a}{x+b}$ the asymptote occurs at $x=-b$, giving $b=4$ and $f(x)=\frac{a}{x+4}$.

steps.title: Use a point on the visible branch to find $a$
steps.content: The curve passes near $(-10,-1)$ on the left branch. Substituting: $$-1=\frac{a}{-10+4}=\frac{a}{-6}\;\Longrightarrow\;a=6.$$ So $f(x)=\frac{6}{x+4}$.

steps.title: Apply the shift $g(x)=f(x+4)$ and match a choice
steps.content: Replace $x$ with $x+4$ in $f$: $$g(x)=f(x+4)=\frac{6}{(x+4)+4}=\frac{6}{x+8}.$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
